skill.name: grilling
skill.description: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design. Use when the user wants to stress-test a plan before building, or uses any 'grill' trigger phrases.
skill.summary: Stress-test plans and designs through one-question-at-a-time review.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The three static weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives in plain-language planning instructions, including one duplicate at SKILL.md line 3. No evidence found of cryptography, code execution, networking, secret access, or prompt injection attempts. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3, but the line is frontmatter prose describing plan and design review behavior. No cryptographic function, library, or algorithm use is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only markdown instructions and frontmatter. The flagged line has no executable code or cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Duplicate Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported a second weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md line 3. This is a duplicate of the same prose-only frontmatter line and does not represent a separate risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The duplicate finding points to the same non-executable description text. No additional evidence supports a security issue.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 6, but the line is a natural-language instruction to question a plan. No cryptographic primitive or unsafe algorithm usage appears in context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is markdown guidance for conducting a planning interview. There is no executable code path or crypto-related behavior.

content.value_statement: Early plans often hide unclear assumptions, missing dependencies, and weak decisions. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to interview the user one question at a time.
actual_capabilities: Runs a focused planning interview about a proposal or design.
Asks one question at a time and waits for feedback.
Provides a recommended answer with each question.
Works through dependencies between planning decisions.
Uses codebase exploration when local evidence can answer a question.
limitations: It does not make final product or technical decisions for the user.
It depends on accurate user answers for context outside the codebase.
It does not replace formal security, legal, or architecture review.
It may need access to relevant project files to answer codebase-specific questions.

use_cases.title: Validate a Feature Plan
use_cases.description: Use the skill before implementation to expose unclear requirements, edge cases, and missing product decisions.

use_cases.title: Review a Technical Design
use_cases.description: Use the skill to examine architecture choices, dependencies, migration paths, and operational risks before coding.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Proposal
use_cases.description: Use the skill to strengthen a written plan before sharing it with stakeholders or reviewers.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Plan Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Grill this plan. Ask me one question at a time and include your recommended answer for each question.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has a rough plan and wants basic pressure testing.

prompt_templates.title: Feature Readiness Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Interview me about this feature plan until the requirements, risks, and dependencies are clear. Start with the highest-impact unknown.
prompt_templates.scenario: A team is deciding whether a feature is ready to build.

prompt_templates.title: Technical Design Interrogation
prompt_templates.prompt: Stress-test this technical design. Explore the codebase when evidence is available, then ask one unresolved question at a time.
prompt_templates.scenario: An engineer wants a design review tied to the current repository.

prompt_templates.title: Decision Tree Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Walk down the decision tree for this proposal. Resolve dependencies between decisions and recommend the next answer after each question.
prompt_templates.scenario: A complex project has many linked product and engineering decisions.

output_examples.input: I want to add a new onboarding flow next sprint.
output_examples.output: First question: What user segment is this onboarding flow for? Recommended answer: Choose one primary segment before designing screens.

output_examples.input: Review my database migration plan before I implement it.
output_examples.output: First question: What is the rollback path if the migration fails halfway? Recommended answer: Define a reversible migration or a tested restore plan.

output_examples.input: Help me decide whether this API redesign is ready.
output_examples.output: First question: Which existing clients must remain compatible? Recommended answer: List every client and define a compatibility window.

best_practices: Provide the plan, goals, constraints, and known decisions before starting.
Answer each question directly so the next question can build on it.
Allow codebase exploration when the answer depends on existing implementation details.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to approve a plan without answering follow-up questions.
Do not use it as a substitute for specialist review in regulated areas.
Do not provide vague goals when concrete constraints are available.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an AI assistant question a plan or design until key decisions and dependencies are clear.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is marked as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it ask many questions at once?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructs the assistant to ask one question at a time and wait for feedback.

faq.question: Can it inspect a codebase?
faq.answer: Yes, when a question can be answered by exploring available project files.

faq.question: Does it create implementation code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on planning questions, recommendations, and decision clarity.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only false positive static findings and no confirmed security issues.
